content:
The project

PAMedios was my main work base for several years in Argentina and remotely. From there I managed communication, content, and campaigns for more than 20 clients with different needs, timelines, and goals.

This experience helped me understand businesses, organize priorities, coordinate profiles, and move work forward with editorial judgment and a strong focus on results.

Context and problem

  • Clients with different goals and messages that needed to be structured.
  • Creative and technical teams that needed to work in sync.
  • The need to turn broad briefs into concrete, publishable pieces.
  • Projects with deadlines, last-minute changes, and multiple channels.

My role

  • Co-founder and communication consultant.
  • Digital content and editorial calendar management.
  • Coordination with designers, web profiles, and other collaborators.
  • Review of visual, editorial, and brand consistency.
  • Campaign metric analysis to suggest improvements.

Working process

  1. Brief intake and goal definition.
  2. Turning business objectives into messages and deliverables.
  3. Organizing content, timelines, and responsibilities.
  4. Coordinating with design, web, photography, and video when needed.
  5. Quality review before publishing or delivery.
  6. Following results and making adjustments.

Coordination and project management

  • More than 20 clients with different needs.
  • Work with designers, web profiles, and creatives.
  • Ongoing communication with stakeholders to align priorities.
  • Managing scope changes, deadlines, and delivery issues.
  • Quality oversight to keep campaigns and pieces consistent.

Strategic decisions and UX

  • Prioritizing messages based on campaign goals.
  • Reducing content when the channel needed immediacy.
  • Adjusting tone and format to audience and funnel stage.
  • Reviewing pieces with clarity, readability, and conversion in mind.

Problems and solutions

  • Problem: briefs were broad and underdefined.
  • Solution: turn them into an action plan with priorities.
  • Problem: too many interlocutors.
  • Solution: organize flows and centralize reviews.
  • Problem: inconsistent output across pieces.
  • Solution: align message, format, and goal before production.

Tools and platforms

  • CMS and web publishing.
  • Content management and editorial calendars.
  • Design and presentation tools.
  • Meta Ads and Google Ads for metrics and optimization.
  • Coordination with creative and web teams.

Result and impact

  • More than 20 clients served.
  • Campaigns, pieces, and content delivered with editorial consistency.
  • Better alignment between communication, design, and business goals.
  • Strong proof of seniority in coordination and strategy.

Visual material

As a visual reference, the Fly Up website helps show the kind of institutional delivery and finish level I was working with.
description: Communication and digital marketing work for different clients, focused on strategy, content, and coordination.
title: PAMedios

content:
The project

La EmmApp is an active web application created to bring together the most important information for one or more pets in one place. It lets users check basic details, vaccines, medication, and upcoming appointments through a cleaner, more current interface.

The project started as part of my web training and I approached it like a product: with a clear problem, a defined user, and a concrete need to simplify daily care management.

Open La EmmApp · View the code on GitHub

Context and problem

  • User: people who need to keep a pet’s health and care under control.
  • Need: bring data, reminders, and appointments into one place.
  • Problem: the information is usually spread across records, notes, and memory.

My role

  • Designed and developed the full application.
  • Made decisions about data structure, flows, and information presentation.
  • Defined the interface so the main view solved the most frequent task.
  • I keep iterating on the experience, code, and visual clarity.

Working process

  1. Identified the problem and the main use case.
  2. Designed the information structure and core flows.
  3. Built the interface and main screens.
  4. Connected registration, editing, and review features.
  5. Reviewed responsive behavior, visual state, and publishing.

Strategic decisions and UX

  • Prioritized the pet overview instead of secondary screens.
  • Organized health information into understandable blocks.
  • Reduced visual noise so the app is quick to understand.
  • Kept the current interface cleaner than the earlier screenshot in the portfolio.

Features

  • Sign up and login with email or Google.
  • Create, view, edit, and delete pets.
  • Image upload.
  • Vaccine and medication tracking.
  • Vet and grooming appointment management.
  • Basic alerts and health states.
  • Pipi-can map on the homepage.

Problems and solutions

  • Problem: health information was scattered.
  • Solution: centralize it in clear pet cards and views.
  • Problem: too many tasks in one app.
  • Solution: prioritize the most frequent navigation and simplify the main screen.
  • Problem: needed a living base.
  • Solution: keep a published version and continue iterating on UI and experience.

Tools

The project uses HTML, CSS, JavaScript, PHP, MySQL, PDO, and Composer. It was developed locally with MAMP, uses GitHub for version control, and has a live public version.

Result and impact

  • App published and accessible online.
  • Interface designed for real use, not just as an academic demo.
  • Clear proposition to keep vaccines, medication, and appointments up to date.
  • Useful example of product thinking, iteration, and current technical judgment.

Visual evidence

The main image shows the real published hero of the application, with the current hierarchy and the three main functional blocks visible in the first screen.

Evolution

  • Continue simplifying forms.
  • Improve accessibility and state messages.
  • Review the mobile experience.
  • Keep expanding the available reminders and resources.
description: Active web app to organize the health and care of one or more pets.
title: La EmmApp

content:
The project

Landing page created for the 3rd Summer School for labor guidance professionals by Orientadora de Orientadores. The page presents a five-day training experience with 19 live sessions, guest professionals, and different topics related to employability and guidance.

The challenge was to turn a large amount of information into a clear, persuasive, and easy-to-browse landing page without losing key details or overwhelming the user. The page had to support acquisition and guide the booking decision.

Visit the 2026 Summer School

Context and problem

  • Client: Orientadora de Orientadores.
  • Need: present an intensive training with lots of content and convert interest into bookings.
  • Problem: too much potentially scattered information for a commercial landing page.

My role

  • Built the landing in WordPress with Elementor.
  • Organized the content architecture and message hierarchy.
  • Decided what needed to appear first to reduce friction.
  • Adapted the page for desktop and mobile to preserve readability and conversion.

Working process

  1. Reviewed the brief and training proposal.
  2. Grouped the content into blocks: proposition, benefits, program, team, FAQs, and CTA.
  3. Defined the reading order to guide the purchase decision.
  4. Implemented the page visually in WordPress and Elementor.
  5. Applied responsive adjustments and final QA before publishing.

Coordination and decisions

  • Worked within the visual identity already defined by the brand.
  • Prioritized linear navigation and quick mobile reading.
  • Simplified blocks to avoid a page that felt long but heavy.
  • Reinforced calls to action where the decision was most likely to happen.

Result

  • Published and live landing page.
  • 19 sessions presented in an orderly way.
  • Program, speakers, and booking terms visible in one experience.
  • A useful example of real delivery with client, content, and UX.

Tools

The project was built with WordPress and Elementor, applying responsive design adjustments and visual content organization.
description: Summer School 2026 landing page built with WordPress and Elementor for Orientadora de Orientadores.
title: Summer School

content:
The project

Elita Tarot will be a single-page landing page designed to communicate a close, trustworthy proposal and make booking a consultation easy.

This project complements the other portfolio pieces because it will be built from scratch without WordPress, using Hugo as the static base, and it will show my own design and frontend decisions.

Context and problem

  • Client: an independent professional with an offer based on trust and close attention.
  • Need: present services clearly and convert visits into contact.
  • Problem: a sensitive offer that needs credibility, the right tone, and a simple structure.

My role

  • Define the one-page structure.
  • Translate the offer into a coherent visual narrative.
  • Prioritize clarity, trust, and mobile conversion.
  • Build the piece with a lightweight, maintainable stack.

Working process

  1. Defined the main message and call to action.
  2. Organized sections to reduce friction.
  3. Designed a simple, direct experience.
  4. Implemented it with Hugo, HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
  5. Prepared it for future visual validation with a real screenshot.

Goals

  • Explain the available services clearly.
  • Convey a warm, intimate, and professional identity.
  • Reduce the steps needed to book a consultation.
  • Deliver a good mobile experience.

Strategic decisions and UX

  • Put more weight on the message and booking than on content volume.
  • Keep a one-page navigation to avoid friction.
  • Think mobile-first because this kind of service is often accessed quickly from a phone.
  • Use a night-style visual tone to reinforce identity and intimacy.

Expected result

  • A light, clear landing page centered on conversion.
  • A useful example of how I define an MVP and make scope decisions.

Planned sections

  1. Intro and call to action.
  2. About Elita.
  3. Types of readings or services.
  4. How a consultation works.
  5. Testimonials.
  6. Frequently asked questions.
  7. Booking via WhatsApp or form.

Tools

The landing will be developed with semantic HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, paying attention to responsive design, performance, and accessibility.

Status

Project in definition phase. The image shown in the portfolio is a provisional visual reference and will be replaced with a real screenshot once the landing is built.
description: One-page landing concept for a tarot professional.
title: Elita Tarot
